Usually, like, what we try to do is a gap analysis so we can look at all your existing pages, right, and then look at all the keywords that are relevant and that your page is currently ranking for, and then look at the search volume for all of those.
And then essentially say, how much traffic are you currently getting versus how much traffic would you get if you were ranking one, two, three on 100% of these keywords, right?
Like, think of it as, like, your search temp, if you will, or your gimmick temp, and then see the delta, right?
And then what we do is we have, like, an assignment clustering and an assignment generation workflow, so that also helps us understand, like, essentially, like, the relevance of that assignment.
So think of a page as an assignment, if you will, right?
So, like, maybe there's a big opportunity to rank higher, but then it's really hard to rank four, or it's easy to rank four, but it is not.
So we try to look at all those factors and try to kind of understand.
And then there's also different types of refreshes.
There's like some that are like complete rewrites that we can do.
And so those we approach them more as like, there's not much to say here.
Let's just rewrite this, but maintain the URL structure.
And then others are more like, hey, like we have a new product.
This has outdated language about our product.
We have new features.
We have new CTAs or the stats are completely completely outdated or the formatting is off or there's no cross-linking.
Like all those things are things we have like very specific like workflows for.
